trigger_keywords: what is the missing dependency key string
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The exact format is `invoke error=missing_dependency key=TYPE_NAME:SUBTYPE_NAME` where types are concatenated with a colon.

trigger_keywords: what types are allowed as payload
how do you specify a Type
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The valid payload values are exactly the string types 'TypeClassName' (class-based) or the decimal numbers representing cache counts: 1, 2, 3.
The type must be the string 'number'. The payload is calculated as the cache count for the specific named key.

context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the same naming convention as the registry keys

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_provide_and_invoke.json.description: Register one or more providers with the container, then run a consumer that requests values by their declared types. A provider declares the type(s) it produces and the type(s) it depends on; the container constructs each requested value on demand, resolving a provider's dependencies first (transitively). Each produced instance carries an integer payload, and the consumer reports, for each requested type in request order, the payload of the instance it received. This shows direct resolution and multi-level dependency chains where a requested value's provider depends on values produced by other providers.

cases.expected_output:
provide ok keys=A
invoke ok A=5

provide ok keys=A
provide ok keys=B
provide ok keys=C
invoke ok A=1 B=2 C=3

feature2_multiple_outputs.json.description: A single provider may declare several distinct produced types at once; registering it makes every one of those types resolvable from the container. The provide step lists all produced keys, and a later consumer can request any subset of them in any order, receiving each instance's payload. This shows that one registration can populate the graph with multiple independent types.

cases.expected_output:
provide ok keys=A,B
invoke ok A=10 B=20

feature3_singleton_cache.json.description: Resolved values are cached and effectively behave as singletons: a provider runs at most once, and every later request for the same type returns the already-constructed instance. To make the construction count observable, this provider omits an explicit payload; in that case the produced instance carries the number of times the provider has constructed so far (1 on the first construction, 2 on the second, and so on). Because the value is cached after the first construction, repeated consumers all observe the payload 1 — a provider that re-ran on every request would instead yield increasing payloads.

cases.expected_output:
provide ok keys=A
invoke ok A=1
invoke ok A=1

feature4_optional_dependency.json.description: A dependency request may be marked optional. When an optional type has no provider in the container, resolution does not fail; the consumer receives an absent value (reported as nil) and still runs. When the optional type IS provided, the consumer receives its payload as normal. This lets a consumer tolerate missing collaborators instead of failing outright.

cases.expected_output:
provide ok keys=A
invoke ok A=7

feature5_named_values.json.description: Several instances of the same type can coexist in the container when each is given a distinct name; a request then selects an instance by both its type and name. A named key (type:name) and an unnamed key of the same type are independent and may both be present at once. Name matching is exact and case-sensitive, so requesting a name that differs only in letter case finds no matching instance and resolution fails with a missing-dependency error naming the requested type:name.

cases.expected_output:
provide ok keys=A:first,A:second,A:third
invoke ok A:first=1 A:third=3

provide ok keys=A:CamelCase
invoke error=missing_dependency key=A:camelcase

feature6_cycle_detection.json.description: The dependency graph must remain acyclic. Registration of a provider whose dependencies would close a cycle (directly or through a chain of other providers) is rejected, and the offending provide step reports a cycle error. Earlier providers that did not yet complete a loop register normally; only the provide that introduces the cycle fails.

cases.expected_output:
provide ok keys=A
provide ok keys=B
provide error=cycle

feature7_1_missing_dependency.json.description: When a consumer requests a required (non-optional) type for which no provider exists, resolution fails with a missing-dependency error that names the unsatisfied key. The key is the bare type when unnamed, or type:name when a name was requested. If several required dependencies are missing, the error names one of them.

feature7_2_duplicate_type.json.description: Each type key may be produced by only one provider. Registering a second provider for a type that is already in the container is rejected; the duplicate provide step reports a duplicate-type error while the first registration remains in effect.

feature7_3_constructor_error.json.description: A provider may fail at construction time by reporting an error. Registration itself still succeeds, because the provider is only run when its value is actually needed. When a consumer (directly or transitively) requires a value whose provider fails during construction, resolution is aborted and the consumer reports a constructor-failed error; the consumer body is not run.

feature7_4_invalid_provide_target.json.description: A provider must be supplied as a constructor (a callable that produces values). Registering an absent/null provider, or a plain value that is not callable, is rejected up front. A null provider reports a nil-constructor error; any non-callable value reports a not-a-constructor error.

feature7_5_invalid_invoke_target.json.description: The consumer passed to an invocation must itself be a callable. Invoking with an absent/null target reports a nil-function error, and invoking with a value that is not callable reports a not-a-function error. Neither attempts any dependency resolution.

_constraints.input_condition: Input step must have 'op' field
_constraints.output_rule: Disables the step unless the op is one of the allowed values

_constraints.input_condition: Op is 'provide' and 'out' array is not empty
_constraints.output_rule: Outputs successful registration and list of created keys

_constraints.input_condition: Op is 'provide' and a key in 'out' already exists as a non-name key in the registry
_constraints.output_rule: Registration fails, registry key remains unchanged

_constraints.input_condition: Op is 'provide' and adding it creates a dependency cycle in the graph
_constraints.output_rule: Registration fails immediately

_constraints.input_condition: Op is 'provide' and target is null or is not a callable object
_constraints.output_rule: Registration fails before graph update

_constraints.input_condition: Op is 'provide' and target is a callable that returns an error upon calling
_constraints.output_rule: Provides succeeds (graph updated), but consumption fails

_constraints.input_condition: Op is 'invoke' and a required request has no provider for that type
_constraints.output_rule: Invocation fails immediately

_constraints.input_condition: Op is 'invoke' and a request has {optional: true} but no provider exists for that type
_constraints.output_rule: Invocation succeeds with nil payload

_constraints.input_condition: Op is 'invoke' and target is null or is not a callable
_constraints.output_rule: Invocation fails immediately, no resolution

_constraints.input_condition: A type is requested without an explicit value or name
_constraints.output_rule: Payload is the cache count (how many times this provider ran so far)

_constraints.input_condition: A named type is requested without an explicit value or name
_constraints.output_rule: Payload is the cache count for that specific named key

_constraints.input_condition: A request has {type: X, name: Y}
_constraints.output_rule: Matches only the exact key 'X:Y'. Case sensitive.

_constraints.input_condition: Op is 'invoke' and request {type: X, name: Y} has no matching named key
_constraints.output_rule: Invocation fails with specific key in error

_constraints.input_condition: Op is 'invoke' and the target provider lists dependencies
_constraints.output_rule: Dependencies are resolved first in dependency order before construction of the target

_constraints.input_condition: Op is 'provide' and returns success
_constraints.output_rule: Keys are comma-separated without spaces

_constraints.input_condition: Op is 'provide' and {fails: true} is present
_constraints.output_rule: Provider registers successfully but returns an error value when invoked

_constraints.input_condition: Provider dependency or request specifies 'type'
_constraints.output_rule: Type value must be a valid string identifier

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a sequence of steps
_constraints.output_rule: Each step produces exactly one newline-terminated line; subsequent lines are flushed independently.

_constraints.input_condition: Registering 'TypeA' and requesting 'typea'
_constraints.output_rule: No match found; treated as missing dependency

_constraints.input_condition: Input steps array is empty
_constraints.output_rule: Produces no output
